title: docs(podman-systemd.unit.5.md): misleading indication on `[Pod]#HostName` key
body:
## Description

The `HostName=` property for `[Pod]` contains `This key can be listed multiple times. ` the following (see bellow)

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7b30d9cd3fdb171e5f65cc9c99ece3c704bbb3e5/docs/source/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.md?plain=1#L1116-L1123

But this seems to be false, as the PodsCreateOption only support string type for hostname

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/5c1ed12d8dcf11afb05b860790addfc26ec1f14d/pkg/domain/entities/pods.go#L86

---

When creating a `.pod` quadlet we have a weird behavior

```ini
[Pod]
HostName="foo"
HostName="bar"
```

Results in 

```
[X-Pod]
HostName="foo"
HostName="bar"

[Unit]
...

[Service]
...
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile=%t/%N.pid --replace --exit-policy stop --hostname "\"foo\"" --hostname "\"bar\"" --infra-name systemd-hostname-infra --name systemd-hostname
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
...
```

We can twice the `--hostname` and running is not raising any error?

```bash
$: podman pod create --hostname "foo" --hostname "bar"
815988e6cf53e59c2206bddd1b322573e8edcca8aceff8bf7b23a1b23ab73339
```

But inspecting the pod give us `Hostname` which is only the last `--hostname` specified

```json
[
     {
          ...
          "CreateCommand": [
               "C:\\Users\\axels\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Podman\\podman.exe",
               "pod",
               "create",
               "--hostname",
               "foo",
               "--hostname",
               "bar"
          ],
          ...
          "Hostname": "bar",
          ...
     }
]
```

The only parsing I can found is here

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/417430c4510729f2f7ea7e4075d7452c7ba03713/cmd/podman/containers/create.go#L490
